subject: PATIENT
heading: POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS:__page_no__12
text: postoperative diagnosis: 1. gastritis. 2. barrett esophagus. 3. normal colon. surgeon: [provider_name] operation: esophagogastroduodenoscopy with biopsy; colonoscopy. description of procedure: patient were taken to the endoscopy room, placed in left decubitus position. mac anesthesia induced by eric spangler, crna. passed the upper olympus scope to the 2nd part of the duodenum. duodenum was normal. pyloric channel patent. gastric mucosa showed some moderate gastritis, photographed and biopsied. on retroflexion, there was no hiatal hernia. on withdrawal of the scope, we noted barrett esophagus. this was photographed and biopsied at all quadrants, involving the distal 1 cm of esophagus. on withdrawal of scope, the body of esophagus and larynx normal. turned the cart around and proceeded with colonoscopy. passed the colonoscope through a very tortuous left colon. we had to apply manual pressure to the panniculus to get the scope to pass. we got the scope all way past the ileocecal valve into the base of the cecum. on withdrawal of the scope, we noted no diverticula, no polyps, no lesions. anorectal area was normal. tolerated procedure well. taken to recovery room in satisfactory condition. assistant: adrienne bedford, np. [provider_name] bsb/modl

text: postoperative diagnosis: 1. gastritis. 2. barrett esophagus. 3. normal colon. surgeon: [provider_name] operation: esophagogastroduodenoscopy with biopsy; colonoscopy. description of procedure: patient were taken to the endoscopy room, placed in left decubitus position. mac anesthesia induced by eric spangler, crna. passed the upper olympus scope to the 2nd part of the duodenum. duodenum was normal. pyloric channel patent. gastric mucosa showed some moderate gastritis, photographed and biopsied. on retroflexion, there was no hiatal hernia. on withdrawal of the scope, we noted barrett esophagus. this was photographed and biopsied at all quadrants, involving the distal 1 cm of esophagus. on withdrawal of scope, the body of esophagus and larynx normal. turned the cart around and proceeded with colonoscopy. passed the colonoscope through a very tortuous left colon. we had to apply manual pressure to the panniculus to get the scope to pass. we got the scope all way past the ileocecal valve into the base of the cecum. on withdrawal of the scope, we noted no diverticula, no polyps, no lesions. anorectal area was normal. tolerated procedure well. taken to recovery room in satisfactory condition. assistant: adrienne bedford, np. [provider_name] bsb/modl
